paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Topic: Should students wear school uniforms? Write a paragraph giving your opinion.

paragraphs.text: I think school uniforms are good because they're cheap and everyone looks the same. Students don't have to worry about clothes and they're easy to wear. Also, you won't need to think about what to wear every day. Many kids like them and it's not bad for families.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Usa 'I think' (evitar en PAU), estructuras simples, vocabulario básico ('good', 'cheap'). Sin conectores sofisticados ni voz pasiva. Contracciones (they're, don't, won't, it's) indican registro informal.

paragraphs.text: School uniforms are widely regarded as a means of promoting equality by eliminating visible socioeconomic differences. Furthermore, a sense of belonging and school identity is fostered when dress codes are applied consistently. Although some argue that self-expression is restricted, the benefits of reduced peer pressure are often considered to outweigh this concern.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B2/C1. Excelente: conectores variados (Furthermore, Although), voz pasiva ('are regarded', 'is fostered', 'are applied'), vocabulario sofisticado ('promote equality', 'socioeconomic differences', 'foster').

paragraphs.text: In my opinion, school uniforms are beneficial because they help students focus on studies rather than fashion. Moreover, they reduce bullying related to clothing and create a more equal environment. However, some students feel that uniforms limit their creativity and personal style in daily life.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Bueno: usa 'In my opinion' (mejor que 'I think'), conectores (Moreover, However), vocabulario intermedio. Falta voz pasiva y construcciones más complejas para nivel C1.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: En la PAU evita siempre 'I think'. Usa conectores de nivel B2+ (Furthermore, Nevertheless, Consequently). La voz pasiva demuestra madurez léxica.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Topic: The impact of social media on teenagers. Present both advantages and disadvantages.

paragraphs.text: Social media's got good and bad things. It's good because you can talk to friends and share stuff. But it's bad because people don't stop looking at their phones. Many teenagers won't leave Instagram and TikTok alone and it's a problem for parents.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2. Estructuras muy básicas, repetición de 'it is good/bad', vocabulario elemental. Contracciones (it's, don't, won't) y registro coloquial. Sin conectores formales.

paragraphs.text: Social media presents both advantages and drawbacks for teenagers. On the one hand, communication is facilitated and access to educational content is enabled by these platforms. On the other hand, excessive use can lead to anxiety and reduced face-to-face interaction. In light of this, balanced usage is widely considered essential.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Perfecto para PAU: introducción neutral, conectores formales (On the one hand, On the other hand, In light of this), voz pasiva ('is facilitated', 'is enabled', 'is considered'), vocabulario preciso.

paragraphs.text: I believe social media has positive and negative effects on young people. The advantages include staying connected with friends and learning new things online. However, the disadvantages are cyberbullying and addiction to screens. In conclusion, teenagers should use social media carefully and with limits.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Usa 'I believe' (evitar en introducción neutral). Tiene conectores (However, In conclusion) pero vocabulario podría ser más sofisticado ('carefully' → 'judiciously').

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Ensayos 'For and Against' requieren introducción NEUTRAL. Usa 'On the one hand/On the other hand'. Concluye con 'Ultimately/All things considered'.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about Remote work: advantages and disadvantages. (140-180 words)

paragraphs.text: I'm sure remote work's very good. It's a nice thing because you stay at home and things are easy. I don't like the office and it's boring for me. Working from home won't make you tired and you can't complain about the traffic.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Gramática deficiente e interferencias ('I'm sure' coloquial). Vocabulario pobre ('good', 'nice', 'things'). Contracciones (I'm, it's, don't, won't, can't) y registro demasiado informal para la PAU.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, professional flexibility is significantly enhanced when tasks are performed remotely. Notwithstanding the potential for social isolation, digital connectivity ensures that collaboration remains seamless. Consequently, overhead costs are reduced while productivity is often maximized. It is widely maintained that this paradigm shift fosters a healthier work-life equilibrium, which is paramount in the modern corporate landscape.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Coherencia con conectores avanzados (Furthermore, Notwithstanding, Consequently). Voz pasiva ('is enhanced', 'are performed', 'are reduced'). Vocabulario académico ('paramount', 'equilibrium').

paragraphs.text: Remote work has many benefits for employees and can save time on travel. Also, I think that people do not have to spend hours commuting to the office. However, it can be lonely sometimes and teamwork is harder. But, in my opinion, the flexibility is very good for families and work-life balance.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Gramática correcta pero conectores simples (Also, But). Evitar 'I think' y mejorar riqueza léxica para nivel C1.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Para lograr el 'Wow Effect' en Andalucía, sustituye conectores básicos por avanzados y usa la voz pasiva para dar objetividad a tus argumentos.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about Should university education be free? (130-150 words)

paragraphs.text: I think university should be free. It's very good for kids and things are easy when you don't pay. People like it a lot because studying's nice and they won't get into debt. Paying for a degree isn't fair and it's bad for poor families.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Vocabulario pobre (good, nice, things). Gramática simple. Contracciones (It's, don't, won't, studying's, isn't) y registro informal. Sin conectores formales.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, the implementation of tuition-free higher education is increasingly regarded as a cornerstone of social mobility. Notwithstanding the initial fiscal burden on the state, talent is effectively harnessed within a meritocratic framework when access is guaranteed. Consequently, national prosperity is significantly enhanced when academic pathways are fully subsidized and decoupled from socio-economic status.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores avanzados (Furthermore, Notwithstanding, Consequently). Voz pasiva ('is regarded', 'is harnessed', 'is enhanced'). Vocabulario preciso ('meritocratic', 'cornerstone').

paragraphs.text: I think that university education should be free because every student deserves an opportunity. Also, many people are not rich enough to pay for degrees and need financial support. However, we must consider that teachers need salaries. But, in my view, the benefits for society are very important and outweigh the costs.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Corrección gramatical adecuada pero conectores simples (Also, But). Evitar 'I think' y mejorar precisión léxica para TOP.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Sustituye el 'I think' por pasivas impersonales y cambia conectores básicos por otros de 'rango C1' para demostrar madurez intelectual ante el corrector.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, pedagogical frameworks are being profoundly reshaped by the integration of digital tools. Notwithstanding the potential for distractions, student engagement is often maximized through interactive platforms. Consequently, access to information is facilitated and educational goals are attained more efficiently. It is widely maintained that technology acts as a catalyst for academic excellence in modern classrooms.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores avanzados (Furthermore, Notwithstanding, Consequently). Voz pasiva ('are being reshaped', 'is facilitated', 'are attained'). Vocabulario académico ('catalyst', 'pedagogical').

paragraphs.text: Technology is very important in modern schools and helps students learn. I think that computers and the internet offer many resources for teachers and pupils. Also, some devices are expensive and not every school can afford them. However, in my view, the role of technology is mostly positive for the future of education.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Gramática correcta pero conectores simples (Also, However). Eliminar 'I think' y mejorar precisión léxica para TOP.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Aplica la regla de las '3Ps' para Murcia: usa Pasivas para el registro, Precisión léxica para el vocabulario y Progresión lógica con conectores C1.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about Climate change: individual vs government responsibility. (150-175 words)

paragraphs.text: I think climate change is really bad and we can't ignore it. People don't do enough good things like recycling and it's a shame. Governments won't fix it alone so we've got to help. Things are hard but they're not impossible if we all try.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Vocabulario simple (bad, good, things). Contracciones (can't, don't, it's, won't, we've, they're) y registro informal. Carece de conectores lógicos y de voz pasiva.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, it is widely maintained that systemic environmental degradation can only be mitigated through robust legislative action. Notwithstanding individual efforts to reduce carbon footprints, government intervention remains paramount to enforce large-scale industrial changes. Consequently, global sustainability goals are attained more effectively when institutional policies are prioritized and collective responsibility is regarded as the cornerstone for long-term ecological restoration.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores avanzados (Furthermore, Notwithstanding, Consequently). Voz pasiva ('is mitigated', 'are attained', 'is regarded'). Léxica rica ('paramount', 'cornerstone').

paragraphs.text: I think that climate change is a big problem for everyone and requires action. Governments should make laws to protect nature and reduce emissions. Also, individuals can help by recycling more and using less energy. However, some people do not care about the environment. But, in my view, we must work together because this situation is very important for the future.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Gramática correcta pero conectores simples (Also, But). Evitar 'I think' para alcanzar TOP en Murcia.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Para dominar la rúbrica de Murcia, sustituye el 'I think' por pasivas impersonales y emplea conectores avanzados que demuestren madurez intelectual.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about the pros and cons of living in a big city. (140-180 words)

paragraphs.text: I think big cities are great. There's loads of nice things and people are happy. But it's not perfect because some things are really expensive and the traffic's awful. I don't mind the noise and the shops can't be beaten. It's a very nice place and I won't move to the countryside.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Vocabulario simple (nice, things). Errores y omisiones. Contracciones (There's, it's, don't, can't, I won't) y registro coloquial. Sin conectores formales.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, urban environments offer unparalleled cultural opportunities and professional prospects. Notwithstanding the escalating cost of living, accessibility to public services is often maximized in metropolitan areas. Consequently, a dynamic lifestyle is fostered, albeit it is widely maintained that noise pollution remains a significant drawback. Overall, the benefits of metropolitan life are frequently considered to outweigh the physiological stressors associated with crowded spaces.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores avanzados (Furthermore, Notwithstanding, Consequently, Albeit). Voz pasiva ('is maximized', 'is fostered', 'are considered'). Vocabulario académico ('unparalleled', 'physiological').

paragraphs.text: Living in a big city has many advantages such as jobs and entertainment. For example, I think there are many opportunities and fun things to do. Also, transport is better than in small towns. However, life is very expensive and sometimes it is too loud. But, in my opinion, if you like meeting new people, a city is a good place to live.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Gramática correcta pero conectores básicos (Also, However, But). Eliminar 'I think' y mejorar precisión léxica para TOP.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Para dominar la rúbrica de Andalucía, sustituye el 'I think' por pasivas impersonales y emplea conectores de rango C1 para guiar la lectura de forma sofisticada.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about the importance of learning foreign languages. (130-150 words)

paragraphs.text: I think languages are really good and it's important for everyone. You can't get a good job without English and things are easier if you speak more. I don't like boring lessons but learning's fun when it's useful. People say it's very good for kids and they won't regret it later.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Vocabulario pobre (good, things). Contracciones (it's, can't, don't, learning's, they won't) y registro informal. Sin conectores lógicos ni voz pasiva.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, the acquisition of foreign languages is increasingly considered a cornerstone of cognitive flexibility. Notwithstanding the prevalence of translation software, genuine linguistic proficiency ensures that cultural nuances are accurately grasped. Consequently, global professional opportunities are significantly enhanced when candidates demonstrate such skills. It is widely maintained that multilingualism acts as a catalyst for cross-cultural understanding in our interconnected society.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores avanzados (Furthermore, Notwithstanding, Consequently). Voz pasiva ('is considered', 'are enhanced'). Precisión léxica ('proficiency', 'catalyst').

paragraphs.text: Learning a new language has many advantages for your life and career. For example, I think that you can find a better job if you speak English well. Also, it is helpful when you travel to other countries. However, studying a language is difficult and takes time. But, in my opinion, it is worth the effort because it opens your mind and broadens your horizons.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Para Valencia, sustituye el 'I think' por pasivas impersonales y emplea conectores de rango C1 para demostrar la madurez discursiva que premia el corrector.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about social media and mental health in teenagers. (130-150 words)

paragraphs.text: I think social media's bad for kids and it's not good for their heads. Things are difficult and people get sad because they don't feel good enough. I don't like all those perfect photos and teenagers won't stop comparing. It's bad for mental health and we can't ignore it.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel A2/B1. Vocabulario pobre (bad, good, things). Contracciones (social media's, it's, don't, won't, we can't) y registro informal. Interferencias y falta de conectores formales.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, the susceptibility of adolescents to virtual validation is frequently argued to be detrimental to their psychological well-being. Notwithstanding the benefits of global connectivity, vulnerable individuals are being constantly exposed to unrealistic standards of perfection. Consequently, professional intervention is necessitated to mitigate the escalating rates of digital anxiety. It is widely maintained that media literacy remains paramount for safeguarding teenage mental health.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores avanzados (Furthermore, Notwithstanding, Consequently). Voz pasiva ('is argued', 'are being exposed', 'is necessitated'). Léxica precisa ('detrimental', 'paramount').

paragraphs.text: I think that social media has many problems for teenagers today. Also, many young people spend too much time looking at screens and they feel lonely. However, it is a good way to talk to friends and stay in touch. But, in my opinion, if parents do not check the apps, kids might have mental health issues like stress or anxiety because of bullying online.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Gramática correcta pero conectores muy básicos (Also, But). Evitar 'I think' y mejorar riqueza léxica para TOP.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Para Valencia, aplica el 'Wow Effect': sustituye conectores básicos por avanzados y cambia el 'I think' por pasivas impersonales para demostrar madurez intelectual.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about Should animals be kept in zoos? (140-180 words)

paragraphs.text: I think zoos are nice and kids love them. They're happy when they see the animals and it's a good day out. But some people don't agree and they say the cages aren't good. paragraphs.text: Furthermore, it is widely maintained that the commodification of sentient beings for public spectacle constitutes a moral failure. Notwithstanding the pedagogical claims made by these institutions, species are being confined to restrictive environments that cannot replicate their natural habitats. paragraphs.text: I think that zoos are a good thing because they help to protect animals that are in danger of extinction. paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Para triunfar en Andalucía, aplica la regla 'C-P-V': Conectores avanzados para la coherencia, Pasivas para el registro y Vocabulario preciso para evitar repeticiones.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph about the impact of tourism on local communities. (150-175 words)

paragraphs.text: I think tourism's really nice for cities and it's good for the shops. People don't mind the crowds and they're happy to buy things. But it's not perfect because there's too much noise and the streets can't stay clean. I won't say it's all bad but we've got to manage it better.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, the socioeconomic fabric of coastal regions is being profoundly reshaped by the seasonal influx of visitors. Consequently, sustainable management strategies must be prioritized so that the community's heritage is preserved. paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Write a paragraph for a formal letter of complaint to a company regarding a faulty product. (130-150 words)

paragraphs.text: I'm really angry because the service was bad and I'm not happy. I bought a thing from u and it's not nice at all. The person in the shop wasn't helpful and things aren't working for me. I don't think I'll buy from you again and I won't recommend you. Bye.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, the lack of professional etiquette displayed during the transaction is considered unacceptable. Consequently, consumer rights must be prioritized to ensure market integrity. It is widely maintained that failing a swift resolution, further legal action will be pursued.

paragraphs.text: I think that your company should improve the customer service. I bought a computer last week but it does not work properly. Also, the delivery was very slow and I had to wait. However, I usually like your products. But in this case, I am very disappointed and I want a solution. I hope you can help me with this problem soon.

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Topic: AI in the classroom. Should technology replace traditional teaching?

paragraphs.text: Actually, many schools are introducing AI and it's good for teachers. Students don't mind using software and the results are nice. But some people won't like it because they're afraid machines will replace humans. So we've got to discuss about how to use AI and not depend of it for everything.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1 con errores. Falsos amigos y calcos: 'Actually' (Currently/Nowadays), 'discuss about' (discuss + objeto), 'depend of' (depend on). Contracciones (it's, don't, won't, they're, we've) y registro mixto.

paragraphs.text: AI in education is a big topic and many teachers use it already. Students like the tools because they get instant feedback and they can practice more. But there are worries about screen time and data privacy. So schools need to find a balance and teachers should train to use AI well.

paragraphs.text: The integration of artificial intelligence into classroom settings has sparked considerable debate. Notwithstanding the benefits of personalised learning, concerns regarding data protection and screen exposure remain valid. Consequently, educators are encouraged to adopt a balanced approach whereby technology supports rather than replaces human interaction. It is widely maintained that digital literacy and critical thinking must be prioritised alongside technical tools.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores (Notwithstanding, Consequently, whereby), voz pasiva y estructuras formales. Vocabulario preciso ('sparked', 'whereby', 'prioritised'). Registro PAU.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: En ejercicios de longitud similar, fíjate en los conectores (Furthermore vs and/but/so), en la voz pasiva y en la ausencia de falsos amigos (actually, discuss about, depend of).

paragraphEvaluator.prompt: Topic: Mental health among teenagers. What role should schools play?

paragraphs.text: The mental health of adolescents is actually a major concern in modern education. Many experts say that schools must offer support and not only academic content. However, the implementation of such programmes is often limited by a lack of resources. It is therefore essential that governments invest in training and in spaces where students can feel safe to talk.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel C1. Conectores (However, It is therefore essential), registro formal. 'Actually' correcto aquí. Estructura clara y vocabulario preciso.

paragraphs.text: Teenagers have a lot of stress and schools should help them. They need someone to talk to and they need time to relax. But many schools only care about exams and grades. So we need to change this and give more importance to wellbeing and not only to marks.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1/B2. Gramática correcta pero conectores repetitivos (and, But, So). Vocabulario básico. Falta variedad de conectores y estructuras formales.

paragraphs.text: Schools are really important for kids' mental health but they can't do it alone. Teachers've got a big job and they must be sensible to students' problems. Lots of young people suffer of anxiety and we need to do an effort to help them. Is necessary to put more resources in this area and we won't fix it without that.
paragraphs.feedback: Nivel B1 con errores. Falsos amigos: 'sensible' (sensitive), 'suffer of' (suffer from), 'do an effort' (make an effort), 'Is necessary' (It is necessary). Contracciones (they've, can't, we've, won't). Registro y precisión léxica fallan.

paragraphEvaluator.strategy: Cuando todos los párrafos miden parecido, descarta por falsos amigos (sensible/sensitive, suffer of/from) y por omisión de sujeto (Is necessary → It is necessary). El mejor suele tener conectores variados y sin errores de registro.

connectorBank.text: Europe is facing a profound demographic transformation as birth rates decline and life expectancy continues to rise. {0}, in several countries, over 20% of the population is now aged 65 or above. {1}, this shift is placing considerable strain on pension systems, healthcare services and labour markets. {2} its challenges, an ageing population also brings opportunities, as older adults increasingly contribute to the economy through part-time work, volunteering and knowledge transfer. {3}, governments must develop inclusive policies that harness the potential of older citizens rather than treating ageing solely as a burden.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Confirma la magnitud del cambio demográfico con datos concretos.
Conector de RESULTADO. El envejecimiento poblacional tiene consecuencias directas sobre los servicios públicos.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('its challenges'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la recomendación política derivada del análisis.

connectorBank.title: Freedom of Speech and Online Platforms
connectorBank.text: The debate over freedom of speech in the digital age has become increasingly polarised. {0}, social media platforms face mounting pressure from governments and civil society organisations to remove harmful content. {1}, defining the boundaries of acceptable speech without infringing on fundamental rights is an extraordinarily complex task. {2} their best efforts, major platforms continue to struggle with disinformation, hate speech and the amplification of extremist views. {3}, a transparent, internationally coordinated regulatory framework may be the only viable path forward.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Confirma y amplía la afirmación sobre la polarización del debate.
Conector de CONTRASTE. Introduce la dificultad de establecer límites sin vulnerar derechos.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('their best efforts'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la única solución viable según el análisis.

connectorBank.title: Sport and National Identity
connectorBank.text: Major international sporting events such as the Olympics and the World Cup have long served as powerful symbols of national identity and collective pride. {0}, victories on the sporting stage can unite entire nations and provide a temporary sense of shared purpose that transcends social and political divisions. {1}, the commercialisation of elite sport and the dominance of a small number of wealthy nations raises questions about fairness and representation. {2} these issues, sport remains one of the few arenas in which people from vastly different backgrounds can find common ground. {3}, fostering inclusive participation in sport at all levels is as important as celebrating elite achievement.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Confirma el poder unificador del deporte con argumentos concretos.
Conector de CONTRASTE. Introduce las tensiones y desigualdades del deporte de élite.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('these issues'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la recomendación equilibrada.

connectorBank.title: Healthcare Innovation and Equity
connectorBank.text: Advances in medical technology and pharmaceutical research have transformed healthcare outcomes in wealthier nations. {0}, life expectancy has increased dramatically and many previously fatal conditions can now be effectively managed or cured. {1}, access to these innovations remains profoundly unequal across the globe, with millions of people in low-income countries still dying from preventable diseases. {2} significant investment from international organisations, fundamental inequalities in healthcare access persist. {3}, ensuring that medical breakthroughs benefit all of humanity, rather than just the privileged few, must be a central goal of global health policy.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Confirma los avances con evidencia concreta sobre la esperanza de vida.
Conector de CONTRASTE. Introduce la cruda realidad de la desigualdad global en salud.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('significant investment'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la obligación moral derivada del análisis.

connectorBank.title: The Psychology of Consumerism
connectorBank.text: Modern consumer culture is built on the premise that purchasing goods and services is a primary source of wellbeing and social status. {0}, advertising and social media fuel a cycle of desire and dissatisfaction that keeps consumption perpetually high. {1}, research in psychology consistently shows that beyond a certain income threshold, additional material wealth contributes very little to happiness. {2} this evidence, the pressure to consume continues to intensify in most developed societies. {3}, encouraging a cultural shift towards experiences, relationships and sustainability over material accumulation is one of the most important challenges of our time.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Confirma el mecanismo psicológico detrás del consumismo.
Conector de CONTRASTE. Introduce la evidencia científica que contradice las premisas del consumismo.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('this evidence'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce el cambio cultural necesario como conclusión del argumento.

connectorBank.title: Biodiversity Loss and Conservation
connectorBank.text: Biodiversity loss is now widely recognised as one of the gravest threats to the long-term stability of life on Earth. {0}, species are currently disappearing at a rate estimated to be between 100 and 1,000 times higher than natural background extinction rates. {1}, economic growth models that prioritise short-term profit continue to drive deforestation, habitat destruction and pollution. {2} the alarming pace of biodiversity loss, there are encouraging examples of successful conservation programmes that have helped to restore endangered species and ecosystems. {3}, a fundamental rethinking of our relationship with the natural world, underpinned by binding international agreements, is urgently needed.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Introduce datos numéricos que confirman la gravedad de la amenaza.
Conector de CONTRASTE. Introduce los factores económicos que perpetúan el problema a pesar de la concienciación.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('the alarming pace'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la necesidad de acción urgente.

connectorBank.title: Work-Life Balance in the Digital Age
connectorBank.text: The boundaries between professional and personal life have become increasingly blurred in the era of smartphones and remote working. {0}, many employees feel pressure to remain constantly available, responding to emails and messages well outside normal working hours. {1}, this always-on culture has been linked to rising rates of burnout, anxiety and reduced productivity. {2} growing awareness of these risks, many companies have been slow to implement meaningful policies to protect employees' personal time. {3}, governments and employers must work together to establish clear digital boundaries that preserve workers' right to disconnect.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Confirma y ejemplifica la difuminación de las fronteras entre trabajo y vida personal.
Conector de ADICIÓN. Añade las consecuencias psicológicas de la cultura del 'siempre conectado'.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('growing awareness'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la recomendación derivada del análisis.

connectorBank.title: The Importance of Critical Media Literacy
connectorBank.text: The proliferation of misinformation and disinformation in the digital age poses a serious threat to democratic societies. {0}, studies show that false information spreads significantly faster on social media than verified news. {1}, critical media literacy — the ability to evaluate sources and identify bias — is rarely taught systematically in schools. {2} a growing recognition of the problem, curricula in most countries have been slow to integrate media literacy as a core skill. {3}, developing citizens who can navigate the information landscape critically and responsibly must be treated as an educational priority.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Introduce evidencia empírica que confirma la gravedad del problema.
Conector de CONTRASTE. Introduce la paradoja: el problema es grave pero la solución educativa brilla por su ausencia.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('a growing recognition'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la necesidad de priorizar la educación mediática.

connectorBank.title: The Future of Work and Automation
connectorBank.text: Automation and artificial intelligence are transforming the world of work at an unprecedented pace. {0}, entire categories of routine and repetitive jobs are being displaced by machines and algorithms. {1}, new roles are emerging in areas such as data analysis, cybersecurity and human-machine interaction that did not exist a decade ago. {2} this technological disruption, the overall impact on employment levels remains contested among economists. {3}, governments and educational institutions must invest heavily in retraining and lifelong learning programmes to ensure that workers are equipped for the jobs of the future.

connectors.explanation: Conector de ÉNFASIS. Confirma la velocidad y el alcance de la transformación laboral.
Conector de CONTRASTE. Introduce el lado positivo — la creación de nuevos empleos — como contrapunto a la destrucción.
Conector de CONCESIÓN. Va seguido de un sustantivo ('this technological disruption'), no de una cláusula.
Conector de RESULTADO/CONCLUSIÓN. Introduce la obligación de preparar a los trabajadores para el futuro.

pauRules.strategyCompetencial: Foco en Cohesión y Mediación. No hay Rephrasing.
pauRules.strategyTraditional: Foco en Use of English. El Rephrasing es clave para el 10.
pauRules.penaltyRule: Regla del 10% (errores ortográficos): 0-2 errores sin penalización; 3 errores = -10% sobre la nota base; 4 = -20%; 5 = -30%, etc. Salirse del límite de palabras implica una penalización adicional del 10%. Score Final = Nota base esperada × (1 − penalización total / 100). Criterios CRUE 2025-2026.
pauRules.notaBaseEsperada: El alumno introduce su nota base esperada (0-10). Las penalizaciones se aplican sobre esa nota: por ejemplo, con nota base 8 y 3 errores ortográficos (−10%), la puntuación final estimada es 7,2.
tips: Simulador: Usa la Nota base esperada para simular tu puntuación. Controla los errores ortográficos: a partir del tercero pierdes un 10% de la nota por cada error.
Simulador: Pega tu redacción en el analizador para comprobar el número de palabras en tiempo real. Si te sales del rango de tu comunidad, se aplica un -10% adicional.
Comunidades competenciales (Valencia, Cataluña, Baleares, Galicia, País Vasco): Foco en cohesión y mediación. No hay ejercicio de rephrasing; prioriza conectores y coherencia.
Comunidades tradicionales (Madrid, Andalucía, Murcia, etc.): El Use of English y el rephrasing son clave para el 10. Practica inversiones, pasiva y transformaciones.
Valencia: En ensayos For and Against la introducción debe ser NEUTRAL (sin opinión personal hasta la conclusión). Límite: 130-150 palabras.
Murcia: Prioriza conectores variados; evita repetir estructuras. Límite: 150-175 palabras.
Andalucía: Se valora especialmente la voz pasiva y las estructuras complejas. Límite: 140-180 palabras.
Madrid: Límite 130-150 palabras. El rephrasing y la corrección gramatical tienen mucho peso.
Estructura: Intro (hook + tesis) → Cuerpo 1 → Cuerpo 2 → Conclusión (resumen + opinión si procede).
Registro: Nunca uses 'I think' ni 'In my opinion' en la introducción. Guarda la opinión para la conclusión.
Léxico: Sustituye palabras baúl (good, bad, important, thing) por alternativas precisas (outstanding, detrimental, paramount).
Presentación: La limpieza y la organización son criterios de evaluación que pueden sumar o restar hasta un 10% de la nota final.
Abreviatura: Usar u, bc, lol en una carta formal implica penalización por no cumplir el registro requerido.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Many teenagers use social media {every day}. They can {share} photos and {discuss about} their problems with friends. Some {actually} spend more time online than with family. This can {affect} their relationships.

errors.explanation: 'Discuss' is transitive — it never takes a preposition. Never 'discuss about'.
False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad'. For 'actualmente' use 'currently' or 'nowadays'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Climate change {poses} a serious threat. Governments must {implement} better policies to reduce {emissions}. {Despite of} warnings, many countries {continue} burning fossil fuels. Scientists say {Is necessary} to act now.

errors.explanation: 'Despite' is never followed by 'of'. Use 'Despite' + noun, or 'In spite of'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is necessary'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Education {plays} a crucial role in society. Many students {assist to} university every year. They work hard {so as} to find a better job. It is a {significant} investment in their future.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Assist' means 'to help', not 'to go to'. The correct word is 'attend'.
'So as' followed by a full clause is incorrect. Use 'so that' + subject + verb.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Technology has changed our lives dramatically. {Actually}, most people own a smartphone. Some argue that we depend heavily on these devices. Experts {recommend to limit} screen time each day.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad'. For 'actualmente' use 'Currently' or 'Nowadays'.
'Recommend' must be followed by a gerund (-ing). Never 'recommend to + infinitive'. Correct: 'recommend limiting'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Unemployment is a serious problem for young people. Many graduates {assist to} job fairs hoping to find work. Governments must create more internship programmes. {Is important} to invest in youth.

errors.explanation: Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is important'.
False friend. 'Assist' means 'to help', not 'to go to'. The correct word is 'attend'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Gender equality {remains} a key issue. Women {often} struggle to reach senior positions despite their {qualifications}. {Actually}, the wage gap still {exists} in most sectors. Many organisations {are agree} that change is needed.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad'. For 'de hecho/en realidad' it works, but here 'In fact' is more appropriate.
Direct translation of 'están de acuerdo'. 'Agree' is a verb — never 'be agree'. Just 'agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Social inequality {affects} millions of people. Governments {are agree} that more investment is {needed}. {Despite of} efforts, many communities remain {marginalised}. Experts {discuss} possible solutions regularly.

errors.explanation: 'Agree' is a verb. Never 'be agree'. The correct form is simply 'agree'.
'Despite' never takes 'of'. Use 'Despite' + noun or 'In spite of' + noun.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Health choices {have} a major impact on wellbeing. {Actually}, obesity rates have {increased} significantly in recent years. Many people {are agree} that diet and exercise are both {essential}. Research {suggests} lifestyle changes help.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad'. For 'actualmente' use 'currently'.
'Agree' is a verb, not an adjective. Never 'be agree'. Just 'agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Many people {enjoy travelling} abroad to learn about new cultures. Travel {gives} us a broader {perspective}. {Eventually}, most travellers return with a greater {appreciation} of their own country. {According to my opinion}, everyone should travel.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Eventually' means 'finalmente/con el tiempo'. For 'posiblemente' use 'ultimately' or 'in the end'.
Redundant Spanglish phrase. The standard expression is simply 'In my opinion'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Education plays a crucial role in society. Students need to {assist to} class regularly to make the most of their learning. {Is essential} that schools provide adequate resources and support for all pupils.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Assist' means 'to help', not 'to go to'. The correct word is 'attend'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is essential'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Technology affects our daily life in countless ways. Social media makes us too dependent on validation. Most experts {are agree} that this affects mental health negatively. Some feel {sensible} about negative comments they receive online.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Sensible' means 'sensato/razonable'. For 'sensible' in the emotional sense use 'sensitive'.
Direct translation of 'están de acuerdo'. In English 'agree' is a verb — never 'be agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Unemployment has a devastating impact on people's lives. Governments must {do an effort} to create more jobs for young people. More needs to be done to address this urgent issue. {In the other hand}, some progress has been made in recent years.

errors.explanation: In English you 'make' an effort, never 'do' one. Make/do is a classic Spanglish confusion.
Direct translation of 'por otro lado'. The correct English phrase is 'On the other hand'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Climate change requires immediate action. Scientists provide us with information about the risks. {At the end}, we all pay the price if governments fail to act. We must {avoid to ignore} the scientific evidence any longer.

errors.explanation: Confusión frecuente. 'At the end' refers to a specific physical or narrative endpoint. For 'ultimately', use 'In the end'.
'Avoid' must be followed by a gerund (-ing), never a to-infinitive. Correct: 'avoid ignoring'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Visual expression remains a powerful way to share creativity. {According to my opinion}, artists should be free to explore any theme. Many people {are agree} that public funding for the arts benefits society as a whole.

errors.explanation: Direct translation of 'según mi opinión'. The correct English phrase is 'In my opinion'.
Direct translation of 'están de acuerdo'. In English 'agree' is a verb — never 'be agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Governments are trying to reduce the carbon footprint of major industries. Many citizens are {interested on} saving the planet. {Is vital} that we invest in renewable energy instead of coal as a matter of urgency.

errors.explanation: Wrong preposition. 'Interested' always takes 'in', never 'on'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is vital'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Students today have many {academic pathways} to choose from. Many of them {assist} to prestigious universities to get a degree. They {enjoy to study} practical subjects like engineering. However, their success depends on their own {dedication} and effort.

errors.explanation: False friend — 'assist' means 'to help', not 'to attend'. The correct verb is 'attend'.
Verb pattern error — 'enjoy' must be followed by a gerund (-ing), never a 'to' infinitive.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Teenagers spend a lot of time seeking {virtual validation} online. They are very {sensible} to comments from their peers. It is difficult to {avoid to look} at the phone every minute. This {influencer culture} affects {mental health} significantly.

errors.explanation: False friend — 'sensible' in English means 'practical/reasonable', not 'sensitive'. Use 'sensitive' for 'sensible' in Spanish.
Verb pattern error — 'avoid' must be followed by a gerund (-ing), never a 'to' infinitive.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: A {sedentary lifestyle} can lead to many {cardiovascular diseases}. Doctors are very {judgemental} and try to help their patients. They give {advices} about {proactive wellness} to everyone who asks.

errors.explanation: ¡Error! "Judgemental" es alguien que juzga a los demás. En este contexto buscamos a alguien con capacidad de escucha y empatía.
Uncountable noun error — 'advice' is uncountable in English and never takes an '-s'. It is always 'advice', never 'advices'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: The {competitive landscape} makes it hard for graduates to find a job. {Informations} about new {internship programs} are available online. Most candidates {are agree} that {job insecurity} is a major problem today.

errors.explanation: Uncountable noun error — 'information' is uncountable and never takes an '-s'. Always use 'information', never 'informations'.
Calco del español 'estar de acuerdo' — 'agree' is a verb in English, not an adjective. Never use 'be agree'. The correct form is simply 'agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Many citizens want to {discuss about} the new border regulations. Some people {are agree} that knowledge about different cultures is beneficial. These policies aim to help social integration across the country.

errors.explanation: 'Discuss' is transitive and never takes a preposition. Never 'discuss about' — just 'discuss'.
Direct translation of 'están de acuerdo'. In English 'agree' is a verb — never 'be agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Globalization has strengthened {global connectivity} worldwide. {Research demonstrate} that economies are becoming more interdependent. {Despite of} the benefits, some {achievement gaps} remain between nations. We must {promote} greater {social cohesion}.

errors.explanation: 'Research' is a singular uncountable noun — it requires the third-person singular '-s'. Always 'research demonstrates', never 'research demonstrate'.
'Despite' is never followed by 'of'. Use 'despite' + noun, or 'in spite of' + noun.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Immigrants arrive looking for a host country to build a new life. {According to my opinion}, cultural diversity enriches societies in many ways. However, integration requires effort from both newcomers and {people is} already living there.

errors.explanation: Direct translation of 'según mi opinión'. The correct English phrase is 'In my opinion'.
'People' is a plural noun and takes a plural verb: 'people are'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: A materialistic society encourages people to buy fast fashion. This leads to a throwaway culture that {harmful} the planet. {Is essential} to reconsider our consumption habits before it is too late.

errors.explanation: 'Harmful' is an adjective; here a verb is needed. The correct form is 'harms'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is essential'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Scientists say that {people is} becoming more unhealthy due to poor diet. Eating processed food daily is detrimental to the heart. Experts {recommend to follow} a balanced diet and exercise routine from an early age.

errors.explanation: 'People' is a plural noun and takes a plural verb: 'people are'.
'Recommend' must be followed by a gerund (-ing). Correct: 'recommend following'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: The use of data privacy settings is paramount today. Many users {dream with} total security online. {Is important} to note that algorithms control what we see and shape our opinions.

errors.explanation: Wrong preposition. In English you 'dream about' or 'dream of' something, never 'dream with'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is important'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: {The important is} to find the right vocational training course. Many students {assist to} careers fairs to explore their options. A skilled teacher can inspire them to succeed and reach their potential.

errors.explanation: Direct translation of 'lo importante es'. In English you need a noun: 'The important thing is'.
False friend. 'Assist' means 'to help', not 'to go to'. The correct word is 'attend'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: We live in a world with many {fasts cars} and new technologies. This causes ecological degradation across the environment. Scientists {are agree} that we must protect endangered species as a matter of urgency.

errors.explanation: Adjectives in English do not take a plural -s. The correct form is 'fast cars'.
Direct translation of 'están de acuerdo'. In English 'agree' is a verb — never 'be agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: {People has} a lot of curiosity about the lives of celebrities. {Actually}, reality television is extremely popular across all age groups. However, we should not take for granted everything we see on screen.

errors.explanation: 'People' is a plural noun and takes a plural verb: 'People have'.
False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad'. Here the intended meaning is 'En efecto' — use 'Indeed' or 'In fact'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Many graduates need to {earn} a high {salary} after university. Finding a {job professional} is {demanding} in today's market. {Is essential} to have a well-written {CV} and good communication skills.

errors.explanation: Wrong adjective order — in English, adjectives come before the noun. It must be 'professional job', not 'job professional'.
Missing subject pronoun — the subject 'It' cannot be dropped in English. Always 'It is essential', never 'Is essential'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Venice is a coastal city with millions of visitors each year. {Actually}, the city is sinking slowly due to rising sea levels. Tourists {enjoy to take} photos of its famous bridges and canals.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad'. For 'curiosamente' or 'de hecho' use 'In fact' or 'Remarkably'.
'Enjoy' must be followed by a gerund (-ing), never a to-infinitive. Correct: 'enjoy taking'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Governments must promote social cohesion across all communities. I {haven't never} seen such extreme inequality in our city. {Is essential} that we make an effort to solve this serious predicament together.

errors.explanation: Double negation. In English two negatives cancel each other out. The correct form is 'have never'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is essential'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Space exploration is a scientific milestone for humanity. Many scientists {are agree} that it benefits life on Earth despite the high cost. {Is widely maintained} that astronomical research ultimately leads to important discoveries.

errors.explanation: Direct translation of 'están de acuerdo'. In English 'agree' is a verb — never 'be agree'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is widely maintained'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: The candidate did not speak slowly enough during the interview. He was not {enough good} for the competitive landscape. {Actually}, he has many valuable skills and strong professional prospects for the future.

errors.explanation: In English, 'enough' follows the adjective it modifies. The correct order is 'good enough'.
False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad', which fits here — but in a formal PAU essay 'However' or 'Nevertheless' would be more appropriate for this contrast.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: {I use to take} the bus to the gym every morning. A sedentary lifestyle is detrimental to psychological well-being. {Is essential} to take part in some physical activity every day to stay healthy.

errors.explanation: 'Use to' is not a valid English form. For a past habit use 'used to'; for a current habit use 'usually take'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is essential'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Scientists have {few time} to stop ecological degradation. We must reduce our carbon footprint by using renewable energy. {Is vital} that governments invest in sustainable alternatives as a matter of urgency.

errors.explanation: 'Time' is uncountable. The correct quantifier is 'little time', not 'few time'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is vital'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Getting a university degree is a great {exit} for many students. They want to earn a high salary in the professional arena. Most experts {are agree} that social mobility depends on equal access to education.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Éxito' in Spanish means 'success', not 'exit'. The correct word here is 'opportunity' or 'route'.
Direct translation of 'están de acuerdo'. In English 'agree' is a verb — never 'be agree'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: The young manager {realized her job} very competently last year. She dealt with many internship programs successfully. {Is widely acknowledged} that job insecurity remains a major concern today.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Realizar' in Spanish means 'to carry out'. 'Realise' in English means 'to become aware of'. Correct: 'carried out her job'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is widely acknowledged'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: I went to the {public bookshop} to find some information for my project. Many researchers {discuss about} the shift towards digital literacy in modern education. It is paramount to gather reliable data carefully.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Biblioteca' in Spanish means 'library', not 'bookshop'. A bookshop sells books; a library lends them.
'Discuss' is transitive and never takes a preposition. Never 'discuss about' — just 'discuss'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: {The actor, that is famous}, appeared on reality television last night. People have a lot of curiosity about his personal life. {Actually}, he earned a lot of money last year and donated much of it to charity.

errors.explanation: In English, relative clauses referring to people use 'who', not 'that', in formal writing. Correct: 'The actor, who is famous'.
False friend. 'Actually' means 'en realidad'. Here the intended meaning is 'Interestingly' or 'In fact'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Many teachers believe that information is helpful for students preparing for exams. They can acquire {valuable knowledges} through academic pathways. {According to my opinion}, this will facilitate their professional prospects in the future.

errors.explanation: 'Knowledge' is uncountable in English and cannot take a plural -s.
Direct translation of 'según mi opinión'. The correct English phrase is simply 'In my opinion'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: It is important to be {with they} when they need help. We should promote social integration and empathy in our communities. {Is essential} that support systems in host countries are strengthened and well-funded.

errors.explanation: 'They' is a subject pronoun. After a preposition you need the object form: 'with them'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is essential'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: We must work {toguether} to improve equality in the workplace. There is a glass ceiling in the professional arena that women face daily. {Is paramount} that we address these inequalities as a matter of urgency.

errors.explanation: Spelling error. The correct form is 'together'.
Missing subject pronoun. In English 'It' cannot be omitted: 'It is paramount'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: My friend {is constipated} because he has a cold. He should rest and {avoid to go} outside in cold weather. Doctors recommend nutritious liquids to support the immune system during recovery.

errors.explanation: False friend. 'Estar constipado' in Spanish means 'to have a cold'. In English 'constipated' is a digestive complaint.
'Avoid' must be followed by a gerund (-ing). Correct: 'avoid going'.

spotTheSpanglish.paragraph: Many people {feel boring} when they have nothing to do at weekends. However, birdwatching is an exciting activity in rural settings. Experts {recommend to try} it at least once before dismissing it as dull.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Solar power is cheap to maintain. ___, renewable energy is becoming more affordable every year.

wrongReasons.However: 'However' introduce contraste — pero las dos frases aquí dicen cosas positivas sobre la energía renovable. No hay contraste, sino adición.
wrongReasons.Despite: 'Despite' requiere un sustantivo o gerundio inmediatamente después, no una cláusula completa. Ej: 'Despite the cost' sí, 'Despite renewable energy is...' no.
wrongReasons.Because: 'Because' introduce una causa, pero aquí no estamos explicando por qué algo ocurre — estamos añadiendo información adicional.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Social media has benefits. ___, it can cause anxiety.

wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' introduce una consecuencia lógica de lo anterior. Pero causar ansiedad no es una consecuencia de tener beneficios — es un contraste.
wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' añade información en la misma dirección. Pero aquí cambiamos de idea — de positivo a negativo. Necesitamos contraste, no adición.
wrongReasons.Since: 'Since' introduce una causa o un punto temporal. Ninguno de los dos sentidos encaja aquí.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ its high cost, electric cars are growing in popularity.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' introduce una cláusula con sujeto y verbo: 'Although they are expensive'. Aquí va seguido de un sustantivo ('its high cost'), así que necesitamos 'Despite'.
wrongReasons.Nevertheless: 'Nevertheless' va entre dos oraciones completas separadas por punto o punto y coma. No puede ir seguido de un sustantivo directamente.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' añade información en la misma dirección. Aquí hay una concesión — algo negativo (el coste) que no impide algo positivo.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Governments must act now. ___, future generations will suffer.

wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' añade más información en la misma dirección. Pero aquí la segunda frase es la consecuencia negativa de NO actuar — no información adicional.
wrongReasons.Whereas: 'Whereas' compara dos situaciones simultáneas y contrastadas. Aquí hay una condición implícita ('si no actúan'), no una comparación.
wrongReasons.Firstly: 'Firstly' introduce el primer punto de una lista. Aquí no hay una enumeración — hay una advertencia sobre las consecuencias.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ the evidence is clear, many people ignore climate change.

wrongReasons.Despite: 'Despite' requiere sustantivo o gerundio: 'Despite the clear evidence'. Aquí va seguido de una cláusula completa ('the evidence is clear'), así que necesitamos 'Although'.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' añade información en la misma dirección. Aquí hay una contradicción — la evidencia es clara pero la gente la ignora.
wrongReasons.Consequently: 'Consequently' introduce una consecuencia. Ignorar el cambio climático no es consecuencia de que la evidencia sea clara — es una paradoja.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Exercise improves physical health. ___, it boosts mental wellbeing.

wrongReasons.However: 'However' introduce contraste. Pero mejorar la salud mental no contrasta con mejorar la salud física — son dos beneficios en la misma dirección.
wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' introduce una consecuencia lógica. Mejorar el bienestar mental no es consecuencia de mejorar la salud física — son dos beneficios independientes.
wrongReasons.Unless: 'Unless' introduce una condición negativa ('a menos que'). No encaja con una frase que añade información positiva.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Technology has transformed education. ___, not all students benefit equally.

wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' añade información en la misma dirección positiva. Pero 'no todos se benefician por igual' matiza o contradice la afirmación anterior — necesitamos contraste.
wrongReasons.Consequently: 'Consequently' indica que lo segundo es consecuencia de lo primero. Que no todos se beneficien por igual no es consecuencia de que la tecnología haya transformado la educación.
wrongReasons.Firstly: 'Firstly' introduce el primer elemento de una lista. Aquí no hay enumeración — hay una matización de la idea anterior.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Obesity rates have risen dramatically. ___, healthcare costs have increased.

wrongReasons.Nevertheless: 'Nevertheless' introduce un contraste o concesión. Pero el aumento de costes sanitarios no contrasta con el aumento de la obesidad — es una consecuencia directa.
wrongReasons.Whereas: 'Whereas' compara dos situaciones simultáneas y diferentes. Aquí hay una relación causa-efecto, no una comparación.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' añade información adicional sin indicar relación causal. Aquí la relación es clara: la obesidad causa el aumento de costes — necesitamos un conector causal.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ urban areas offer more jobs, rural regions provide a better quality of life.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' introduce una concesión — algo que no impide lo que viene después. Aquí no hay concesión sino una comparación directa entre dos realidades distintas. 'Whereas' es más preciso para ese contraste paralelo.
wrongReasons.However: 'However' va entre dos oraciones separadas. No puede encabezar una frase subordinada que compara dos ideas en paralelo dentro de la misma oración.
wrongReasons.Despite: 'Despite' requiere sustantivo o gerundio: 'Despite offering more jobs'. Aquí va seguido de una cláusula completa con sujeto y verbo.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Young people must develop digital skills. ___, they should not neglect critical thinking.

wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' indica que lo segundo es consecuencia de lo primero. Pero no descuidar el pensamiento crítico no es consecuencia de desarrollar habilidades digitales.
wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' añade información en la misma dirección. Pero aquí hay una matización — las habilidades digitales son importantes PERO no hay que descuidar otras. Necesitamos contraste.
wrongReasons.Since: 'Since' introduce causa o tiempo. Ninguno de los dos sentidos encaja en esta frase.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ working long hours, many employees feel unproductive.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' + cláusula con sujeto y verbo: 'Although they work long hours'. Aquí va seguido de un gerundio ('working'), así que se necesita 'Despite'.
wrongReasons.However: 'However' va entre dos oraciones separadas. No puede encabezar una frase seguida de un gerundio como 'working long hours'.
wrongReasons.Whereas: 'Whereas' compara dos sujetos distintos. Aquí hay una sola situación paradójica — trabajar mucho y sentirse improductivo.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The government invested in education. ___, exam results improved significantly.

wrongReasons.On the other hand: 'On the other hand' introduce la perspectiva opuesta. Pero los mejores resultados en los exámenes no son la perspectiva opuesta a invertir en educación — son su consecuencia.
wrongReasons.In contrast: 'In contrast' compara dos realidades diferentes. Aquí no hay contraste — hay una relación causa-efecto clara.
wrongReasons.Nevertheless: 'Nevertheless' introduce algo que sucede a pesar de un obstáculo. Aquí no hay obstáculo — hay una causa y su efecto positivo.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ some argue technology isolates people, others see it as a tool for connection.

wrongReasons.Despite: 'Despite' requiere sustantivo o gerundio: 'Despite some arguments'. Aquí va seguido de una cláusula completa con sujeto y verbo ('some argue...').
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' añade información en la misma dirección. Aquí se presentan dos posturas opuestas — necesitamos un conector de contraste.
wrongReasons.Consequently: 'Consequently' indica que lo segundo es consecuencia de lo primero. Pero que otros vean la tecnología como herramienta de conexión no es consecuencia de que algunos argumenten que aísla.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Artificial intelligence offers great potential. ___, ethical concerns must be addressed.

wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' añade información positiva en la misma dirección. Pero los problemas éticos no son otro beneficio de la IA — son una advertencia o matización.
wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' indica consecuencia. Las preocupaciones éticas no son consecuencia del potencial de la IA — son una advertencia independiente.
wrongReasons.Unless: 'Unless' introduce una condición negativa. No encaja con la estructura de esta frase.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Immigration enriches culture. ___, it can create social tensions.

wrongReasons.As a result: 'As a result' indica que lo segundo es consecuencia de lo primero. Las tensiones sociales no son consecuencia de que la inmigración enriquezca la cultura.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' añade más información en la misma dirección positiva. Pero crear tensiones sociales es un punto negativo — necesitamos contraste.
wrongReasons.Provided that: 'Provided that' introduce una condición: 'siempre que'. No encaja con la estructura de esta frase.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ you study consistently, your PAU results will improve.

wrongReasons.Despite: 'Despite' introduce una concesión, no una condición. La frase dice que SI estudias, mejorarás — es una condición, no algo que sucede a pesar de un obstáculo.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' añade información adicional. No puede encabezar una cláusula condicional con sujeto y verbo.
wrongReasons.Whereas: 'Whereas' compara dos realidades simultáneas y contrastadas. Aquí hay una condición — algo que ocurrirá si se cumple una premisa.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Fast food is convenient. ___, it contributes to obesity.

wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' añade información en la misma dirección. Pero contribuir a la obesidad no es otro beneficio de la comida rápida — es un efecto negativo que contrasta con su conveniencia.
wrongReasons.Firstly: 'Firstly' introduce el primer elemento de una lista. Aquí no hay enumeración — hay una concesión seguida de una advertencia.
wrongReasons.Whereas: 'Whereas' compara dos sujetos distintos en paralelo. Aquí hablamos del mismo sujeto (la comida rápida) desde dos ángulos distintos.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ the benefits of social media, cyberbullying remains a serious issue.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' introduce una cláusula con sujeto y verbo: 'Although social media has benefits'. Aquí va seguido de un sustantivo ('the benefits'), así que necesitamos 'In spite of' o 'Despite'.
wrongReasons.However: 'However' va entre dos oraciones separadas con punto. No puede encabezar una frase seguida de un sustantivo.
wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' indica consecuencia. El ciberacoso no es consecuencia de los beneficios de las redes sociales.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Students must read widely. ___, they should practice writing regularly.

wrongReasons.However: 'However' introduce contraste. Pero practicar la escritura no contrasta con leer — son dos actividades complementarias en la misma dirección.
wrongReasons.Consequently: 'Consequently' indica consecuencia. Practicar escritura no es consecuencia de leer ampliamente — son dos recomendaciones independientes.
wrongReasons.Whereas: 'Whereas' compara dos realidades distintas y contrastadas. Aquí no hay contraste — hay dos recomendaciones que se complementan.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Many species are endangered. ___, deforestation continues at an alarming rate.

wrongReasons.On the contrary: 'On the contrary' introduce la idea opuesta o la negación de lo anterior. Pero la deforestación no contradice que las especies estén en peligro — lo agrava.
wrongReasons.Provided that: 'Provided that' introduce una condición. No encaja con una frase que añade información adicional sobre un problema ambiental.
wrongReasons.Nonetheless: 'Nonetheless' introduce algo que ocurre a pesar de un obstáculo. Aquí no hay obstáculo — hay una acumulación de problemas ambientales.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: She studied medicine for six years. ___, she became a highly respected surgeon.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'As a result' signals that becoming a surgeon is a direct consequence of six years of study — a clean causal chain that scores highly in PAU writing.

wrongReasons.In contrast: 'In contrast' would imply that becoming a surgeon contradicts studying medicine, which is illogical.
wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' introduces a concession — it would mean she became a surgeon despite studying, not because of it.
wrongReasons.Unless: 'Unless' is a negative conditional. It would mean she would not become a surgeon if she had not studied — a different structure entirely.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The report highlighted serious flaws in the system. ___, no action was taken.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Yet' is a concise, sophisticated connector that flags a paradox — serious flaws were identified, but nothing happened. Ideal for a data-driven PAU argument.

wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' would mean inaction is a logical conclusion of the flaws, which makes no sense.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' adds information in the same direction — it would mean inaction is another feature alongside the flaws.
wrongReasons.As a result: 'As a result' implies inaction was caused by the report, which reverses the intended logic.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ the rain, the match went ahead as planned.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Despite' is followed directly by a noun ('the rain'). It signals concession in a compact, formal structure — more elegant than 'although' for PAU writing.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' requires a full clause with subject and verb: 'Although it was raining'. Here the noun 'the rain' follows, so 'Despite' is correct.
wrongReasons.However: 'However' connects two separate sentences. It cannot begin a clause mid-sentence before a noun phrase.
wrongReasons.Because of: 'Because of' introduces a cause. Here the relationship is concessive — the match happened in spite of the rain, not because of it.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The economy has grown steadily. ___, unemployment remains high.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Nevertheless' acknowledges the growth and then introduces the unexpected contradiction — high unemployment. This concessive structure is a hallmark of mature PAU writing.

wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' adds information in the same direction. It would imply unemployment is another positive alongside growth.
wrongReasons.Consequently: 'Consequently' would mean unemployment is caused by economic growth — the opposite of the intended meaning.
wrongReasons.In addition: 'In addition' treats unemployment as a further positive feature, which contradicts the logic.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ she failed the first exam, she passed the course with distinction.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Although' introduces a subordinate concessive clause with its own subject and verb. It signals that the positive outcome happened in spite of the initial failure.

wrongReasons.Because: 'Because' would mean passing with distinction was caused by failing the first exam — logically backwards.
wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' signals a conclusion. Passing because of failing is not a logical conclusion.
wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' adds information in the same direction. It cannot connect a setback with a positive outcome.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Air quality has improved in cities. ___, respiratory diseases remain the leading cause of hospital admissions.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Nonetheless' (formal synonym of 'nevertheless') concedes the improvement and pivots to the persistent problem. Sophisticated and typical of C1 academic writing.

wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' would imply respiratory diseases are caused by the air quality improvement — illogical.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' adds a point in the same direction, not a contrasting one.
wrongReasons.As well as: 'As well as' connects parallel items mid-sentence. It cannot join two independent clauses.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: You can borrow the car ___ you return it by midnight.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Provided that' introduces a positive condition — permission is granted on one specific condition. More formal and precise than 'if' for PAU writing.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' is concessive, not conditional. It would mean you can borrow it despite returning it late.
wrongReasons.Nevertheless: 'Nevertheless' is a contrast connector. It cannot introduce a condition.
wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' adds information. It cannot express a condition.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The new policy aims to reduce carbon emissions. ___, it includes stricter penalties for polluters.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Furthermore' adds a second feature of the policy in the same direction — both clauses describe positive aspects of the same initiative.

wrongReasons.However: 'However' would signal a contradiction — implying stricter penalties undermine the policy's goal.
wrongReasons.Unless: 'Unless' is a negative conditional. It cannot connect two positive statements about the same policy.
wrongReasons.Despite: 'Despite' requires a noun or gerund immediately after. It cannot introduce a full clause here.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ working overtime every day, she never managed to meet the deadline.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Despite' + gerund ('working') is the correct structure here. It signals that the effort was real but insufficient — a concise, formal construction.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' requires a full clause: 'Although she worked overtime'. 'Despite' takes a gerund directly.
wrongReasons.However: 'However' connects two separate clauses. It cannot begin a gerund phrase.
wrongReasons.Because: 'Because' introduces a cause. Here the relationship is concessive — she failed despite the effort.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The government increased the education budget. ___, exam results did not improve.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'However' is the standard concessive pivot: increased investment should improve results, but it did not. This unexpected contrast is exactly what examiners look for.

wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' would mean poor results were caused by increased spending — the opposite of the intended logic.
wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' adds a point in the same direction. It cannot introduce a contradiction.
wrongReasons.As a result: 'As a result' implies the bad results were a direct consequence of the investment — illogical.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Streaming services have transformed the entertainment industry. ___, cinemas continue to attract large audiences.
connectorSpeedMatch.explanation: 'Nevertheless' concedes the transformation and then pivots to the surprising resilience of cinemas. This is a sophisticated PAU-style contrast.

wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' would mean large cinema audiences are another sign of streaming's success — contradicting the logic.
wrongReasons.Therefore: 'Therefore' implies cinema attendance is a result of streaming's rise — illogical.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The athlete trained for months. ___, a last-minute injury prevented her from competing.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Many experts argue that social media is harmful. ___, it is now an essential tool for communication.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The study was thorough and well-funded. ___, its conclusions remain controversial.

wrongReasons.However: 'However' connects two separate sentences. It cannot begin a prepositional phrase.
wrongReasons.Whereas: 'Whereas' draws a parallel contrast between two subjects. Here there is only one subject — renewable energy.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: Students must develop critical thinking skills. ___, they will struggle to evaluate information independently.

wrongReasons.Furthermore: 'Furthermore' adds a point in the same direction. It cannot introduce a negative consequence.
wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' is concessive. It would mean students struggle despite developing critical thinking.
wrongReasons.Despite: 'Despite' requires a noun or gerund immediately after. It cannot begin a full independent clause.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: ___ urban areas offer better career prospects, rural regions provide a higher quality of life.

wrongReasons.Although: 'Although' introduces a concession — one idea yielding to another. 'Whereas' simply contrasts two coexisting facts without one overriding the other.
wrongReasons.Nevertheless: 'Nevertheless' connects two separate sentences. It cannot begin a subordinate clause.
wrongReasons.Moreover: 'Moreover' adds information in the same direction. It cannot signal a contrast.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: You will receive the scholarship ___ you maintain a GPA above 8.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: The city has excellent public transport. ___, traffic congestion remains a daily problem.

connectorSpeedMatch.sentence: She had never visited the country before. ___, she felt completely at home from the first day.

Learning the local language is a significant step toward finding a job.
Moving to a new country provides many people with a better life opportunity.

bubbles.wrongTopic: Vago: Nunca uses 'things' o 'stuff' en un ensayo académico.
